reasoning: The current position can be calculated as the sum of the initial position and the product of velocity and time. We can verify this by changing one variable at a time and observing the effect on position.
Increasing time resulted in a corresponding increase in position, confirming the position is a function of time and velocity. Next, I will increase velocity to observe its effect.

The position has increased proportionally with both velocity and time, suggesting the equation is position = initial_position + velocity * time. No need to change initial_position as it remains constant.
